content: metrics_daily (date, metric, dimension, value) uses a composite PK on (date, metric, dimension) with dimension NOT NULL DEFAULT '' so the nightly rollup upserts idempotently via ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET value = excluded.value. Dimensioned metrics (e.g. signups by role) store one row per dimension; the period total is the SUM across dimension rows — never a separate '' total, which would double-count.

name: pure-metric-definitions
description: Spec §10 metric definitions live as pure, DB-free helpers in src/lib/analytics/metrics.ts (resolvePeriod, computeTrend, computeFunnel, activeBuyerWindows, metaHealthySince, median) that take an explicit now:Date; DB query fns feed them counts. Makes the 'unambiguous numbers' requirement unit-testable without Postgres. track()/rollup follow the same split: rollupEvents(date, events) is pure, runDailyRollup wraps it with query+upsert. DB fns take injectable Db=defaultDb (mirrors listing-stats repo.ts). Counts use sql<number>`count(*)::int` and aggregate filters.

name: nightly-rollup-worker
description: metrics-rollup BullMQ queue (worker/processors/metrics-rollup.ts) wraps analytics-core runDailyRollup with three jobs: repeatable 'sweep' (00:30 UTC tz-pinned via METRICS_ROLLUP_CRON) rolls up previousUtcDay; 'rollup-day' rolls one explicit day; 'backfill' fans out a rollup-day per day in [start,end] inclusive, capped at MAX_BACKFILL_DAYS. Idempotency is inherited (composite-PK onConflictDoUpdate); empty days no-op to metrics:0. Follows the listing-expiry sweep pattern. Pure date helpers (previousUtcDay/enumerateUtcDays reuse utcDayBounds) + processMetricsRollup take injectable deps (db/now/enqueueRollupDay) for Redis/Postgres-free unit tests via a fakeDb seam. Adding a queue breaks the exact-set assertions in src/lib/queue/jobs.test.ts and worker/queue.integration.test.ts — update both.

name: server-side-funnel-instrumentation
description: Wire spec §10 funnel events by awaiting the analytics named helpers (trackListingView/trackInquirySubmitted/trackSignup/trackListingCreated/trackSave) at each server mutation or SSR point. track() already wraps every write in try/catch and swallows, so awaiting is safe and non-blocking. NEVER call track with a transaction handle inside the mutation's own db.transaction: a failed INSERT aborts the whole pg tx even though track swallows the JS error, which would roll back the user's action. Fire analytics AFTER commit, in the route/hook. listing_view is written on the SSR page with UA bot-filtering (isLikelyBot); inquiry_started has no server mutation so it goes through POST /api/analytics/beacon.

title: save_count = total list_items, bumped via raw SQL in the save transaction
rationale: OVYRO-e58f: chose the total-list_items definition (each list_item counts) over count-distinct-per-user because it is the direct analog of view_count/lead_count and is race-safe — every add/remove independently adjusts the counter, so concurrent saves to different lists can't double-count the way a read-modify-write distinct check would. Increment sits inside addItemToList's db.transaction only when a row is actually inserted (onConflictDoNothing makes same-list re-saves idempotent); decrement inside removeItemFromList only when a row is deleted. Uses raw SQL (sql`... save_count = save_count + 1 ...`) exactly like incrementListingView so a save does NOT trip listings.updated_at and needlessly bust the R-8 cache. GREATEST(save_count - 1, 0) guards underflow from list_items predating the counter. addItemToList now returns {item, created} so the route fires the save event only on genuine first saves.

name: analytics-beacon-endpoint
description: Client-only funnel events (inquiry_started) go through POST /api/analytics/beacon. navigator.sendBeacon-friendly: reads the body as text() then JSON.parses leniently (sendBeacon sends a Blob so content-type is unreliable), validates with a strict Zod allow-list (only inquiry_started; bounded id lengths) so an unauthenticated endpoint can't mint arbitrary events, IP rate-limits with failOpen:true (a Redis hiccup must not drop real funnel data), attributes to getActor() best-effort (no auth required), and ALWAYS returns 204 — a beacon response is discarded and a bad/dropped event must never surface as a console 4xx on the visitor's page. Pure parsing/IP helpers live in _lib/payload.ts (zod-only) so they unit-test without the DB pool. Client fires it from the inquiry form's useEffect on mount, wrapped in try/catch with a keepalive fetch fallback.

Phase 5.5 integration verify: /admin/analytics (spec §4.1.5) has no page.tsx — never existed in git history — yet admin-nav.tsx:25 and admin/page.tsx:50 both link to it, so a logged-in admin gets a 404. The series.ts chart-helper lib (src/app/admin/analytics/_lib/series.ts) shipped and is unit-tested, but the page consuming it was never built. Not a §14 v1 blocker (the §14 admin item is the /admin overview KPI reconcile, which passes).
resolution: Reported to coordinator + filed OVYRO-bba8 for a follow-up build; did NOT fix-forward (full §4.1.5 time-series charts suite is feature work, larger than a verify-pass fix).

name: phase5-integration-verify-fresh-db
description: Reproducible Phase 5.5 verification on a FRESH Postgres (ovyro_p5v3): drizzle-kit migrate creates 19 tables incl. metrics_daily (additive); seed + seed:demo + seed:dev; next build + next start; exercise real HTTP actions with a curl cookie jar (sign-up/email, /land/[slug] SSR view, PUT/DELETE saves, POST inquiry) to confirm analytics_events writes; save_count inc/dec + double-save idempotent; bot UA (Googlebot) filtered from listing_view; analytics resilience proven live by ALTER TABLE analytics_events RENAME away mid-save (save still 201, error swallowed to console/Sentry). Rollup idempotency via processMetricsRollup rollup-day run twice (composite-PK upsert, no dup). KPI reconcile: call loadAdminOverview(30) and diff every headline KPI against independent raw COUNT SQL — all matched. Suspend→sign-in 403 ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED→unsuspend→200; admin_audit_log gets user.suspend/unsuspend/role.grant. zsh gotcha: never name a loop var 'path' (tied to PATH); use /usr/bin/curl.
